description: Tasks for validating repomind on a canonical flat Python package. The 'requests' library keeps all source in a single requests/ directory with ~15 files. This is the textbook flat-package layout: no domain subdirectories, no subpackages. Expected: repo_shape='flat-package', flat_rescue activates, keyword-based feature groupings produced (auth, models, sessions, adapters).

expected_classification.feature_count: >=2 (flat_rescue should produce auth, sessions, adapters at minimum)
expected_classification.avg_feature_confidence: >=0.3 (heuristic-only; lower bar acceptable for flat repos)
expected_classification.notes: The repo has no subdirectories — flat_rescue.py is the only classification path. Keyword groupings from _HIGH_DOMAIN_TOKENS should produce auth, models, sessions.

tasks.description: Fix a bug in how requests handles HTTP authentication headers
tasks.notes: requests/auth.py — should be grouped into an 'Auth' or 'authentication' feature by flat_rescue

tasks.description: Change how requests builds and sends HTTP sessions
tasks.notes: requests/sessions.py — large central file; likely entry point

tasks.description: Update the HTTP adapter for connection pooling
Add a new field to the requests Response model
tasks.notes: requests/models.py — contains Request and Response classes

tasks.description: Debug cookie jar handling in requests

interpretation.pass_bar: repo_shape='flat-package'; at least 2 features detected by flat_rescue; tasks 001-005 achieve >=50% coverage
interpretation.fail_signal: 0 features and 0 modules (flat_rescue failed); or crash; or coverage 0% on all tasks
interpretation.known_challenge: Without a graph.db, all classification is keyword-based. Confidence will be low. The bar for flat repos is lower: correctness over confidence.
interpretation.comparison_with_own_repo: The repomind repo itself is NOT flat-package (it has code_review_graph/memory/ as a subpackage). requests/ is the canonical flat-package case.
